content.body: bkardell: that never existed though. Those are probably broken links to https://www.w3.org/TR/html-design-principles/
content.formatted_body: bkardell: that never existed though. Those are probably broken links to https://www.w3.org/TR/html-design-principles/

content.body: Fetching while in parallel in general is not something I would recommend. Preparing a fetch requires all kinds of global state.
%JSON.parse% needs a realm. This is a known issue: https://github.com/whatwg/infra/issues/625
I think some specs prepare the request based on a global state and then pass those parameters along to the parallel processing to be fetched later
ah weird it is actually a bad w3c redirect
links to http://dev.w3.org/html5/html-design-principles/#priority-of-constituencies redirect to the url I shared. I'll let them know
Even that link seems somewhat suspect, but maybe it was hosted in the same repository at some point. I guess that's plausible. Hard to fathom we worked on that almost 20 years ago now.
